pr.title: Fix itthunder by removing unnecessary constants, mark as matching
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Removed unnecessary global float constants from itthunder, converted the relevant assignment to a literal 0.0f, changed two sdata2 entries from global symbols to local compiler-style labels, and marked src/melee/it/items/itthunder.c as Matching in configure.py.
what_changed: In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, it_804DD3A8 and it_804DD3AC were changed from global .sdata2 float symbols to local labels @149 and @175.
In src/melee/it/items/itthunder.c, it_802CCC68 now assigns item->xDD4_itemVar.thunder.x68 = 0.0f instead of assigning the named constant it_804DD3A8.
In src/melee/it/items/itthunder.h, the definitions f32 it_804DD3AC = 1.0f and f32 it_804DD3A8 = 0.0f were removed.
In configure.py, melee/it/items/itthunder.c was moved from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
smart_moves: Recognized that the float constants did not need project-level global names and should remain local .sdata2 constants in the symbol map.
Used the literal 0.0f in C source rather than forcing a fake global data dependency for it_804DD3A8.
Removed actual variable definitions from the header, avoiding misleading or unnecessary header-owned storage for constants.
Updated the matching status only after the source and symbol cleanup made itthunder.c match.
decomp_lessons: Do not promote every .sdata2 float to a global symbol; if the original object treats a constant as local, represent it as a local @NNN label in symbols.txt.
For simple scalar constants such as 0.0f, a literal in C may match better than referencing a named global constant.

Headers should not define decomp-only float constants unless there is clear evidence they are real shared objects; prefer extern declarations only for real globals, or no declaration for local constants.
When a nonmatching item file only differs due to unnecessary constant materialization, fixing the constant model can be enough to mark the object Matching.
naming_conventions: Real global item symbols use names like it_804DD378 or it_804DD3B8 when they are meant to be globally addressable.
Local compiler-generated constants in config/GALE01/symbols.txt use @NNN labels such as @149 and @175 instead of it_804Dxxxx global names.
Item source paths follow the melee/it/items/it<item>.c convention; this PR concerns itthunder.c and itthunder.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace artificial global float references with immediate/literal C constants when that reflects the original codegen.
Keep symbols.txt storage classification aligned with matching needs: local .sdata2 labels for local constants, global names only for true globals.
After symbol and source cleanup, update configure.py from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...) for the matched translation unit.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only available human signal is the title, which states the intent: remove unnecessary constants and mark itthunder as matching.
follow_up_queries: Find other item headers that define f32 constants directly instead of using literals or proper extern declarations.
Search symbols.txt for it_804Dxxxx globals that are only local float constants and may need conversion to @NNN labels.
Check other NonMatching item files for artificial constant globals blocking a match.
